# Transcrição: 02-How Do I Find My Avatar

## [00:00 - 03:03]

All right, everybody, Russ Hennaberry here, and we're going to be talking about how to find and determine your customer avatar. And a lot of you are going to already know who your avatar is. You just know instinctively maybe you've been working with the product that you're selling for a long time or the company that you're selling for and you understand who this persona is. But I wanted to shoot a quick video for you guys to start to think about some different ways you could segment the market and figure out where some avatars might live, where some places are that you might say, yes, there's an entire group there that I want to segment and speak to differently than this other group. And that's really the test that we're looking for is, you know, is this group of people that aren't enough from who I'm ordinarily reaching that I want to create a separate persona for them. I want to speak to them differently. You know, as we keep going through this module, you're going to be talking about things like outcomes and transformation and keep this part in mind that I'm teaching you here as you start to think about outcomes and what outcomes people want from the products they buy from you and services that they buy. Because if the outcome is different enough, it probably ties back to one of these things, which would be demographics. So you might, you know, you might have a business where you're writing ads where demographics matter. You might, you know, it might be that you're going after people of retirement age, like nearing retirement or already in retirement, you might be going after particularly young folks with an offer. And you know, that might mean that you need to speak to them differently in the copy. They're going to have different outcomes that they want, etc. Another one might be their vocation. So like, what do they do for a living professionally? So you might, you might have a product that makes sense to go business to business and you're trying to reach CMOs, so chief marketing officer. And then you also realize, wow, actually there's a there's a whole nother outcome that someone might want from this product or service in sales. And so let's create a separate persona and talk differently to the person in charge of the sales department at companies. Or it might be their interest, right? So they might, you know, it might be something that this person's into photography and they're interested in portrait photography, right? So they want to take pictures of their family, they want to take pictures of portraits, whereas you have a whole not, you realize, wait a minute, there's a whole nother sub-interest

## [03:03 - 06:03]

in here that might want to buy this product that I'm selling of people that are interested in nature photography or food photography or whatever it is. So it's a different interest that causes you to say, okay, there's a different persona here, we're going to have different befores and afters, we're going to have different transformation they want, we're going to have different challenges and goals and different things like that. So we need to speak to them differently. And that is what causes you to split out and say, okay, here's a new segment here I'm trying to reach. It could be a life event, right? So maybe let's go back to that photography example. If you're selling photography as a service, there's going to be different life events that you would want to target. So it might be weddings is one type of persona that you're going after. And then you're also going after people that have just recently had a baby, right? And speaking to them differently because of different goals, different challenges, different things they want. It might be values, right? So you have people that want the product because they want to maybe they're very Christian or religious in some way. And then you've got a whole another group of people who has a totally separate value system. They may not be like super into religion, but they might be super into family values and making sure that their family grows up well and everybody's taking care of it. And those are related, but this is a different enough value set that you're going to want to speak to them differently in your ad copy and in the way that you talk to them. It could be their goals, right? So it could be what is it that they're trying to achieve? And then the last one is challenges, right? So challenges being what is stopping them from getting those goals, right? What is what is holding them up? So, you know, these are different ways that you might approach scaling your ads because you might say, well, look, I'm kind of tapped out with this demographic. I've, you know, run these ads to people that live in this area that are over the age of 65 and we're sort of, you know, hitting a wall here and we need to scale. And you might look at the demographics and say, oh, there's a different there's a different persona here. Or you might look at vocation and say, there's a different way to talk to people and that's going to open up a whole new group of people for me so I can scale up. So those are the different ways I want you to be considering segmentation and deciding, you know, if you do need another persona, another person to talk to, these are good ways to segment. All right, so how do you find these people and how do you research them and where do you look? Well, you know, one of the things you can do is you can ask yourself because you are a member of that group, that segment, that persona.

## [06:03 - 09:09]

And you just know, you know what the goals and the challenges are and the things that we're going to be asking you as we move forward in this module, you know how to answer them because you are a member of that segment. And that happens a lot because we build businesses sometimes or work on things where we have passion and, you know, we have the same problems as the person we're trying to reach. So this is common. If you're not a member of the market, you can go and ask members of a group, right? So you can go in and attend to play, attend things and listen and talk to people that are members of this group. The third thing I would tell you, and this is pretty tactical here, but works really, really well, is that you go into Amazon and you spy. And the way that I do this is you're going to find a book. I don't care what you're selling. If you can find a book that is connected to the outcome of whatever it is you sell. And then later I'll come back and organize it in a way I can use it. But right now I'm just trying to get voice to the customers, trying to pull it out of here and get it in some usable format.
